_meta.questionsPerSection: variable — Section 1 has 7 questions (Q01–Q05, Q26, Q27); Sections 2–5 have 5 questions each

weights.note: Physical body and core digestion/emotion/sleep questions carry weight 2. Lifestyle and behavioural questions carry weight 1.

_meta.notes: Each option maps to a dosha (V/P/K). Weighted tally at end determines dominant dosha(s). Thresholds are based on weighted total of 41 — see prakritiConstants.ts for TRIDOSHIC_MAX_SPREAD and DUAL_MAX_SPREAD.

sections.title: Physical Body
sections.description: Your natural physical traits — the body you were born with, not current condition

questions.text: How would you describe your natural body frame?
questions.hint: Think about how you've been built since childhood, not recent changes

options.text: Thin and light — I find it hard to gain weight even when I eat well
Medium and athletic — I gain and lose weight with moderate effort
Broad and sturdy — I gain weight easily and lose it slowly

questions.text: What does your skin naturally tend to be like?
questions.hint: Without skincare products — your natural default

options.text: Dry, rough, or flaky — especially in cold or windy weather
Oily, warm, or prone to redness, acne, and sensitivity
Smooth, thick, and naturally moist — rarely dry or irritated

questions.text: How would you describe your hair naturally?
questions.hint: Your hair's natural texture before any treatments

options.text: Dry, thin, frizzy, or prone to breakage and split ends
Fine, straight, tends to go gray or thin early, oily scalp
Thick, wavy, lustrous, and naturally well-nourished

questions.text: What do your joints and bones feel like?

options.text: Prominent, sometimes crack or pop — joints feel delicate
Moderate and flexible — rarely an issue unless inflamed
Large, well-padded, and sturdy — rarely bothered by joint issues

questions.text: How do your eyes generally appear?

options.text: Small, nervous or darting, dry — blink frequently
Medium, sharp, penetrating, light-sensitive, reddish sometimes
Large, calm, deep, moist with long lashes — naturally attractive

questions.text: How does your body temperature naturally tend to run?
questions.hint: Think about your baseline — not when sick or in extreme weather

options.text: Cold — my hands and feet are often cool, I need more layers than others in the same room
Warm or hot — I run warmer than most and feel uncomfortable in heat
Moderate — I rarely feel extreme cold or heat and adapt to temperature reasonably well

questions.text: What is your natural sweating pattern?
questions.hint: Consider moderate activity — a walk, light work — not extreme exercise

options.text: Minimal — I barely sweat even during exercise, with little or no body odour
Profuse — I sweat easily and heavily, with a strong or pungent odour
Moderate — I sweat a reasonable amount with a mild or pleasant odour

sections.title: Digestion & Appetite
sections.description: How your digestive system naturally behaves

questions.text: How would you describe your appetite and hunger patterns?

options.text: Irregular — I sometimes forget to eat, hunger comes and goes unpredictably
Strong and intense — I get irritable or get headaches if I skip meals
Steady but low — I can easily skip meals without much discomfort

questions.text: How is your digestion and bowel movement naturally?

options.text: Irregular, tendency toward constipation, dry or hard stools, gas/bloating
Fast and efficient — sometimes loose, diarrhea when stressed, heartburn
Slow and steady — bowel movements are regular but unhurried

questions.text: What food tastes and temperatures do you naturally crave?

options.text: Sweet, salty, sour foods — warm, cooked, and oily comfort foods
Sweet, bitter, astringent — cool or raw foods, love spicy but it doesn't love me
Pungent, bitter, astringent — light, dry, and stimulating foods

questions.text: How do you feel after a heavy meal?

options.text: Bloated or gassy — heavy meals make me uncomfortable and anxious
Fine usually — maybe some heartburn but energy remains decent
Heavy and sluggish — I feel like sleeping after a big meal

questions.text: How do you respond to cold weather or cold food and drinks?
questions.hint: Think about both: how your body feels in a cold climate AND how you feel after cold food or drinks

options.text: I dislike cold intensely — it makes me stiff, anxious and uncomfortable
I prefer cold — it cools me down and I feel better in cooler temperatures
I tolerate cold reasonably but prefer warm — cold can make me congested

sections.title: Sleep & Energy
sections.description: Your natural energy rhythms, stamina, and sleep patterns

questions.text: How is your sleep quality and pattern naturally?

options.text: Light and interrupted — I wake easily, have vivid dreams, or struggle to fall asleep
Moderate — I fall asleep easily but sleep fewer hours and wake feeling alert
Deep and long — I love sleep, can sleep anywhere, hard to wake up in the morning

questions.text: How do you feel when you wake up in the morning?

options.text: Tired and anxious — takes time to feel grounded and ready to face the day
Alert and ready — I'm up quickly and my mind is already planning the day
Groggy and slow — I need time (and possibly coffee) before I feel functional

questions.text: How is your physical stamina and endurance?

options.text: I start strong with bursts of energy but tire quickly — I need frequent rest
Good moderate stamina — I can push hard but overheat or burn out under pressure
Excellent sustained endurance — I'm slow to start but can go for a long time

questions.text: What time of day do you naturally feel most alive and energetic?

options.text: Early evening — I get a second wind after 5pm and my creativity peaks at night
Midday — I'm most productive, sharp, and driven from morning to early afternoon
Morning after warming up — once I get going mid-morning I have steady energy

questions.text: How do you recover after illness or physical exertion?

options.text: Slowly and inconsistently — I recover in fits and starts, need lots of rest and warmth
Fairly quickly — I push through recovery and get impatient if healing takes too long
Gradually but steadily — I take time but once recovered, I'm fully well again

sections.title: Mind & Emotions
sections.description: Your natural mental tendencies, emotional patterns, and temperament

questions.text: How do you naturally learn and process new information?

options.text: I grasp things very quickly but forget them just as fast — short attention span
Sharp and focused — I learn with precision and retain what I learn with good memory
Slow to learn but deeply retentive — once I know it, I never forget it

questions.text: How do you typically respond to stress?

options.text: I get anxious, scattered, and fearful — I overthink and worry excessively
I get irritable, critical, and controlling — I want to fix the problem immediately
I become withdrawn and avoidant — I internalize stress and can get depressed

questions.text: Which emotion do you struggle with most naturally?

options.text: Fear and anxiety — worry about the future, change, and uncertainty
Anger and frustration — when things don't go my way or meet my standards
Attachment and grief — I hold onto people and things, find it hard to let go

questions.text: How would close friends describe your personality?

options.text: Enthusiastic, creative, spontaneous, full of ideas — but sometimes flaky or unpredictable
Driven, intelligent, decisive, natural leader — but sometimes intense or perfectionist
Warm, loyal, patient, nurturing — but sometimes stubborn or resistant to change

questions.text: How do you handle change and new situations?

options.text: I love variety and get bored with routine — but too much change overwhelms me
I adapt quickly as long as I feel in control — I dislike being micromanaged
I prefer stability and routine — change is stressful and I need time to adjust

sections.title: Lifestyle & Behaviour
sections.description: Your natural behavioral tendencies, social patterns, and lifestyle habits

questions.text: How do you naturally speak and communicate?

options.text: Fast, talkative, enthusiastic — I jump between topics and speak with my hands
Sharp, precise, and to the point — I make my arguments clearly and persuasively
Slow, measured, and thoughtful — I choose my words carefully and speak calmly

questions.text: How do you approach money and finances?

options.text: I spend impulsively on things that excite me — saving is a real challenge
Strategic and goal-oriented — I spend on quality and invest deliberately
Conservative and careful — I save consistently and dislike financial risk

questions.text: How would you describe your walk and physical movement?

options.text: Quick and light — I move fast, fidget, can seem restless or hurried
Purposeful and brisk — I walk with direction and don't like to waste time
Slow and graceful — I move deliberately and unhurriedly

questions.text: How do you approach work and tasks?

options.text: I love starting things but struggle to finish — I work in bursts of inspiration
I'm driven and efficient — I set high standards and am competitive with myself
Methodical and reliable — I'm slow to start but I always complete what I begin

questions.text: How are you in social situations naturally?

options.text: I love meeting people and get excited in groups — but I can feel drained after
Confident and engaging — I naturally take charge and enjoy healthy debate
Warm and supportive — I prefer deep one-on-one connections over large groups

resultThresholds.pureDosha: One dosha scores >= 60% of weighted total (~25+ points)
resultThresholds.dualDosha: Two doshas within 7 weighted points of each other
resultThresholds.tridoshic: All three doshas within 5 weighted points of each other
